page.content: In assessing the operational efficiency and inventory management of General Motors' manufacturing plants across Europe, the data presented in Table 1 and Table 2 provides critical insights into the current market conditions. The utilization rate of the plants stands at an impressive 95.0%, indicating robust production capabilities amid fluctuating demand.

data presented in Table 1 and Table 2 provides critical insights into the current market conditions. The utilization rate of the
plants stands at an impressive 95.0%, indicating robust production capabilities amid fluctuating demand.

page.content: Furthermore, the financial health of the organization is reflected in the P/E Ratio, which has risen to 17.1, suggesting a favorable market perception. Additionally, the Current Ratio of 1.7 indicates adequate liquidity to meet short-term obligations. Meanwhile, the Debt/Equity ratio of 0.45 portrays a conservative leverage strategy that supports sustainable growth.

lines.text: Furthermore, the financial health of the organization is reflected in the P/E Ratio, which has risen to 17.1, suggesting a
favorable market perception. Additionally, the Current Ratio of 1.7 indicates adequate liquidity to meet short-term obligations.

page.content: Turning to dealer operations, the inventory dynamics in Northern Europe are particularly noteworthy; for instance, DealerID 001 maintains a stock level that corresponds to a Days Supply of CHF36,000, reflecting effective inventory management practices. In contrast, DealerID 004 operates within a similar framework, ensuring that supply levels are aligned with regional demand fluctuations. By closely monitoring these metrics, General Motors can effectively adapt its manufacturing strategies and dealer inventory management to optimize performance across the European market.
